items.title: SEC filing: 8-K — Fermi Inc. (CURRENT REPORT)
items.formExplanation: A current report — the form companies use to disclose material events between quarters (deals, leadership changes, results, financings).
items.summary: Fermi Inc. commenced an offering for the sale of $350 million aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2031 to qualified institutional buyers, with the option to purchase an additional $52.5 million. The company intends to use the net proceeds to pay for capped call transactions and for general corporate purposes. Fermi Inc. also disclosed that it has deferred its election to be taxed as a real estate investment trust, and now expects to make this election for its taxable year ending December 31, 2026.

items.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
items.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, Vicksburg Investments Management LLC, and other parties have withdrawn their solicitation to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders due to an untenable timeline caused by a judge's recusal in pending litigation. The special meeting was intended to address entrenching actions taken by the company's directors, but the parties will not act on any agent designations received from shareholders unless the solicitation is resumed. The withdrawal comes after the parties had filed a definitive proxy statement and accompanying agent designation card with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

items.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
items.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has suspended his current proxy campaign to call a special meeting of shareholders due to a judge's last-minute recusal, which has disrupted the timeline for the strategic process. Neugebauer's campaign had earned the backing of over 70% of votes cast and two leading proxy advisory firms, and he remains confident in Fermi's ability to secure a tenant group and achieve long-term success. Neugebauer will continue to push for good governance through the judicial process and monitor the board's execution on Project Matador to ensure maximum value for stakeholders.

items.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., disclosed that the company has dropped its lawsuit against him, abandoning its effort to validate a 70% supermajority bylaw amendment, after a Texas court ordered the company to produce documents and testimony explaining the rationale for the bylaw. Neugebauer's claims challenging the validity of the bylaw remain alive and he will continue to pursue the truth through discovery and depositions, which he believes is necessary for good governance and to lower the company's cost of capital. The development is significant as leading proxy advisory firms Glass Lewis and Egan-Jones have recommended that shareholders consent to th

items.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
items.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder of Fermi America, delivered an investor presentation on June 30, 2026, expressing concerns about the company's contracts, including the EPC contract and gas contract, which he believes could be either assets or liabilities. Neugebauer emphasized the importance of having the right team to steward these contracts and ensure the company's success, and he advocated for a dual-track process and evidence-based decision-making in evaluating potential tenants. Neugebauer also stated that he and his team are not trying to come back to the company, but rather want to ensure that the board prioritizes the interests of shareholders.

items.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
items.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer and other participants, including Vicksburg Investments Management LLC and Melissa A. Neugebauer 2020 Trust, have filed a definitive proxy statement with the SEC in connection with soliciting agent designations for a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. The filing discloses that Mr. Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of Fermi Inc.'s common stock, while the other participants do not beneficially own any shares. The participants are soliciting support for calling a special meeting of shareholders to be held as promptly as practicable.

items.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
items.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, the co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., is hosting a Town Hall webinar to answer questions from analysts and provide clarity and transparency to shareholders, after receiving support from two leading proxy advisory firms, Glass Lewis and Egan-Jones, to call a special meeting of shareholders. The webinar is scheduled for June 30, 2026, and Neugebauer has invited Fermi leadership, including Governor Rick Perry and Co-President Jacobo Ortiz, to join and provide answers, although they have so far declined. Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of Fermi's common stock and is soliciting agent designations to call a special meeting to empower shareholders

items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer and affiliated entities have filed a definitive proxy statement with the SEC in connection with the solicitation of agent designations for calling a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of Fermi's common stock, and is advising all shareholders to read the definitive proxy statement and other proxy materials because they contain important information. The filing discloses the Participants' efforts to call a special meeting of shareholders as promptly as practicable, with the proxy materials available at no charge on the SEC's website.

items.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
items.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has announced that the Texas Business Court granted his motion for expedited discovery, requiring Fermi to provide documentation and testimony regarding its recent actions, including the imposition of a 70% supermajority voting requirement. This development is a significant win for Neugebauer and Fermi's shareholders, who have been seeking greater accountability and transparency from the company's board. Neugebauer will also host a Town Hall webinar with analysts on June 30 to address questions and provide updates on his efforts to restore "good governance" to Fermi.

Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of Fermi's common stock, while the other participants do not own any shares. The filing advises shareholders to read the definitive proxy statement and other proxy materials for important information regarding the solicitation.

items.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
items.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest individual shareholder of Fermi America, Inc., has proposed a town hall webinar for June 30th where he, Governor Rick Perry, and other parties will answer questions from analysts and journalists about the company's past, present, and future. Neugebauer is seeking to give shareholders a say in the company's direction, particularly regarding Project Matador, a 7,500-acre private power campus in Texas, and whether to pursue a strategic dual-track process to maximize shareholder value. The proposed webinar comes after Governor Perry and the board gave themselves a 70% supermajority vote threshold, making it difficult for shareholders to change course.

items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest individual shareholder of Fermi Inc., has issued a press release and letter to shareholders expressing confidence that a tenant deal will be announced soon and calling for a Special Meeting of Shareholders to restore normal governance and evaluate all potential strategic transactions. Neugebauer and other participants are soliciting agent designations to call the Special Meeting, where shareholders can vote on Board composition and potentially elect new, independent board nominees. The move is seen as an effort to maximize shareholder value and ensure that all strategic alternatives are honestly evaluated, amid concerns about the current Board's gov

items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has issued a press release responding to recent media inquiries and SEC filings, calling on the Fermi Board to maximize shareholder value and find a new leader to replace the nine senior leaders who departed after his termination. Neugebauer highlights his team's accomplishments during his tenure, including securing a 99-year ground lease and over $1 billion in financing facilities, and emphasizes the need for transparency and accountability in determining Fermi's future. The press release comes after significant positive stock momentum in response to Neugebauer's recent open letter, which outlined a dual path of tenant ac

items.title: SEC filing: DEFA14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO SECTION 240.14A-12)
items.formExplanation: Additional definitive proxy material from the company — its side of an ongoing solicitation (responses, letters to shareholders, talking points).
items.summary: Fermi Inc is urging shareholders to sign and return the WHITE Consent Revocation Card in response to former CEO Toby Neugebauer's consent solicitation to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders, citing Neugebauer's pattern of false claims and inaccurate statements. Neugebauer is seeking to replace the Board and leadership team with his own nominees, which Fermi's Board believes would jeopardize shareholder value and benefit Neugebauer and his affiliates at the expense of other shareholders. The company is highlighting its commercial momentum and progress under new management, and warning shareholders not to sign or return any GREEN Consent Cards sent by Neugebauer.

The Fermi Founder Parties, who beneficially own approximately 146.5 million shares of Fermi Inc. common stock, are seeking to call a special meeting to allow shareholders to hear proposals and make informed decisions. items.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, along with other participants, has issued a press release reaffirming his support for John Sellers of Double Eagle as Chairman and CEO of Fermi, citing the need for enhanced accountability and credibility to prevent value destruction by the current Board and management team. Neugebauer claims that the current Board has failed to replace nine key senior executives who departed Fermi after his termination and has ignored proposals from large companies that could offer greater value to shareholders. The release also highlights Neugebauer's intent to continue his campaign to seat a new, strong, independent Board and restore Fermi's position as a pro-shareholder REIT.

items.title: SEC filing: DEFA14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO SECTION 240.14A-12)
items.formExplanation: Additional definitive proxy material from the company — its side of an ongoing solicitation (responses, letters to shareholders, talking points).
items.summary: Fermi Inc is mailing a Consent Revocation Statement to shareholders in response to a consent solicitation by former CEO Toby Neugebauer, who is seeking to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders. The company's Board of Directors urges shareholders to sign and return the WHITE Consent Revocation Card to protect their investment and support the company's continued execution of its Fermi 2.0 strategy. Neugebauer, who was terminated for cause, currently does not have the required support to call a Special Meeting, and the company emphasizes that it is making progress on its projects, including Project Matador, and is focused on maximizing long-term shareholder value.
